sections.heading: Theming in Jetpack Compose
sections.content: Jetpack Compose uses MaterialTheme to provide colors, typography, and shapes consistently across the composable tree. Material3 (Material Design 3) introduces dynamic color on Android 12+, updated components, and type-safe Kotlin-based theming, eliminating XML styles and attribute lookups.

sections.heading: Why Material3 Theming?
items: Consistent design with automatic theme token usage
Dark mode support via light/dark ColorSchemes
Dynamic color on Android 12+ from wallpaper
Type safety prevents typos in color/typography tokens
Single source of truth: change theme once to update all components
Customizable via CompositionLocal for subsections
Override individual tokens without full theme replacement
Material You support with updated components

rows.material2: primary, primaryVariant, secondary, background, surface, error
rows.material3: Expanded palette including tertiary, tonal surfaces

sections.heading: Setting Up a Custom Material3 Theme
sections.content: Create a composable AppTheme that wraps MaterialTheme with light/dark ColorSchemes, Typography, and Shapes. Use dynamic color on Android 12+, and wrap all app content inside AppTheme for consistent theming.

sections.heading: Theme.kt Structure
items: Define LightColorScheme and DarkColorScheme with brand colors
Create AppTheme(darkTheme = isSystemInDarkTheme(), dynamicColor = true) { MaterialTheme(...) { content() } }
Apply in MainActivity: setContent { AppTheme { NavHost(...) } }
Use Material Theme Builder to generate full color schemes

rows.description: Main brand color for actions, FAB

sections.heading: Using Theme Colors in Composables
items: Text: color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurface
Background: Modifier.background(MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surface)
Icon tint: tint = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary
Card: CardDefaults.cardColors(containerColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surfaceVariant)
Button: ButtonDefaults.buttonColors(containerColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.secondary)
Never hardcode colors; always reference MaterialTheme tokens

sections.heading: Custom Typography & Fonts
sections.content: Define a Typography object with custom TextStyle roles. Add font files in res/font, create a FontFamily, assign it to TextStyle, and pass Typography to MaterialTheme. Use Google Fonts Compose library if needed.

sections.heading: Custom Shapes
items: Define Shapes object with extraSmall, small, medium, large, full
Pass to MaterialTheme: MaterialTheme(shapes = AppShapes)
Access in composables: Surface(shape = MaterialTheme.shapes.medium)
Override per component if needed with shape parameter

sections.heading: Dynamic Color & Dark Mode
items: Use dynamicLightColorScheme() / dynamicDarkColorScheme() on Android 12+
Detect dark mode: isSystemInDarkTheme()
Wrap content: AppTheme(darkTheme = isSystemInDarkTheme()) { ... }
Compose Material3 components adapt automatically to color scheme

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Material3 in Compose provides a type-safe, dynamic, and consistent UI system. Define light/dark ColorSchemes, Typography, and Shapes, wrap your app in AppTheme, use dynamic color on Android 12+, extend with CompositionLocal for custom tokens, and always reference MaterialTheme tokens to ensure dark-mode-ready, consistent UI.
